name: Coast Guard Sector Houston-Galveston
tagline: The Coast Guard guarding the Houston Ship Channel and the upper Texas coast.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Houston-Galveston (Galveston Island + Houston metro, TX; Central time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA TX282 Houston, which covers the Galveston/Houston area; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Guards the busy Houston Ship Channel + Gulf SAR/interdiction. Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Texas moved to TRICARE West in 2024. No state income tax. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 77554 (Galveston).

data.Role: Coast Guard hub for the upper Texas coast
data.Mission: Port security for the Houston Ship Channel, search and rescue, and interdiction
data.Setting: Gulf Coast (Galveston Island + the big Houston metro)
data.Note: Texas has no state income tax

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Houston-Galveston guards one of the nation's most important energy and shipping corridors, the Houston Ship Channel, along with search and rescue and law enforcement across the upper Texas coast.
Units span Galveston Island and the greater Houston metro, pairing Gulf-Coast beach access with the amenities (and growth) of one of the country's largest cities, and no state income tax.

sections.title: Island or Metro? Galveston vs. Greater Houston

data.subtitle: The main choice is beachside Galveston versus the big, affordable-for-its-size Houston metro, depending on which units you're tied to and what lifestyle you want.
intro: Galveston Island offers beach-town living; the Houston metro offers big-city amenities and a wide range of affordable suburbs. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families rent.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools, with Galveston for the coast and the mainland (League City, Clear Lake, the Bay Area) for value and schools.

recommendations.advice: Galveston Island puts you on the Gulf; weigh coastal insurance and hurricane risk.
Mainland suburbs like League City, Friendswood, and the Clear Lake area offer good schools and more space.
The greater Houston metro adds amenities and an international airport a drive inland.
It's the Gulf Coast; budget for coastal insurance and know your evacuation plan.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in the area is limited, so most families rent on Galveston Island or across the Houston metro.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA TX282 (Houston, covers Galveston) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Galveston ISD, Clear Creek ISD (League City/Clear Lake), and other Houston-area districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; most family care is via the TRICARE network and Houston-area hospitals (a major medical region)

data.Commissary: Nearest DoD commissary is at Ellington Field (Houston area)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the affordable-for-its-size Houston metro (no state income tax) makes buying a real option, especially on the mainland.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the island versus the metro before committing.
You'd rather not take on coastal insurance and storm risk on the island.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat Houston-area assignments.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable mainland market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. The mainland Houston metro is affordable with no income tax, so buying can pencil out; the island carries higher coastal-insurance and storm risk. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, factor in insurance, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on Galveston versus the mainland (League City/Clear Lake) before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Texas vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Galveston ISD or Clear Creek ISD) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; Houston is a major medical region.

data.a: It's the Coast Guard's hub for the upper Texas coast, guarding the busy Houston Ship Channel plus search and rescue and interdiction across the Gulf.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. It's MHA TX282 (Houston, which covers Galveston). Texas also has no state income tax.
On Galveston Island for the beach, or across the mainland Houston metro (League City, Clear Lake) for value and schools. CG housing is limited.
It's the Gulf Coast, so budget for coastal insurance and know your evacuation plan, especially on the island.
